problem.title: Container With Most Water
problem.description: Given n non-negative integers height where each represents a point at coordinate (i, height[i]), find two lines that together with the x-axis form a container that would hold the maximum amount of water.

example.explanation: Lines at index 1 and 8 form container with area min(8,7)*(8-1) = 49.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Two Pointers [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Start with the widest possible container using two pointers at both ends. At each step move the pointer with the shorter height inward, since the area is limited by the shorter wall and increasing width is no longer possible. This greedy choice guarantees we never miss the maximum area.
